summarization.a: The chart displays the intensity of X-ray diffraction peaks at various 2θ angles, indicating crystalline phases of two materials: GeTe and Sb2Te3.

data_extraction.a:
| 2θ  | Intensity (au) GeTe| Intensity (au) Sb2Te3|
|---|---|---|
| 20 | - | -|
| 27 | 021 peak | 009 peak|
| 28 | - |015 peak |
| 30 | 202 peak |-|
| 38 | - |1 0 10 peak |
| 43 | 024 peak |110 peak|
| 45 | 220 peak |0015 peak|
| 52 | Si peak |205 peak|
| 63 | - |1 0 19 peak|

a.question: Do both materials show similar crystal phases?
a.answer: a	Most peaks do not overlap, however de 220 and 110 peaks should belong to similar crystal phases.

a.question: What is the most prominent peak for Sb2Te3?
What is the substrate on top of which material is grown and which film is likely thicker?
a.answer: a	The used substrate is silicon. This can be seen by the silicon peak in GeTe, the visibility of this peak also indicates that this film is thinner.

a.question: Are the materials single or poly-crystalline?
